summary: Ukrainian forces struck Russian positions on the southern front with FPV drones, destroying an observation post, fortified shelter, mortar position, and antenna equipment, killing most personnel. Border units also used FPV drones to target Russian forces before striking, and in one case, two drones hit a Russian soldier hiding behind a tree simultaneously. Meanwhile, Ukraine’s drone procurement surged, with twice as many Mavic, Autel, and Matrice multirotors contracted in January–February compared to last year, and deliveries up 17% in February. On the Russian side, the "Yolka" air defense drone performed a kinetic intercept of a Ukrainian fixed-wing UAV, and a fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley" destroyed a Ukrainian MaxxPro armored vehicle in Dobropole. Russian operators from the "Rubikon" Center reported 104 strikes on Ukrainian positions across multiple directions on March 11, 2026. Ukrainian forces are also deploying increasing numbers of "Molnias" (lightning drones) and strike/reconnaissance UAVs, though electronic warfare systems struggle to counter them due to unknown control frequencies.
